title: TAIH Symposium 2026
text:
Welcome to the TAIH Symposium 2026, hosted by the Transdisciplinary Artificial Intelligence Hub (TAIH). This inaugural event brings together researchers, practitioners, and industry leaders to discuss the latest advancements, transparent methodologies, and real-world applications in AI and connected research fields.
For more information about TAIH, visit our website.
Date: Summer 2026 [TBD] Location: University of Calgary, Alberta, Canada

title: TAIH Symposium 2026
text:
About the symposium
Machine learning and artificial intelligence are increasingly deployed in high-stakes domains. However, ensuring trustworthiness, transparency, and accountability remains a critical challenge. This symposium aims to advance research by exploring robust methodologies, interpreting model-driven decisions, and ensuring reliability in real-world use cases.

title: TAIH Symposium 2026
text:
Confirmed speakers
The symposium includes a number of talks by invited speakers. Details will be announced soon.

title: Sponsorship Opportunities
text:
We are actively seeking academic and corporate partners for the TAIH Symposium 2026. Sponsoring our event provides an excellent opportunity to showcase your organization to leading researchers and top-tier students at the University of Calgary.

Why sponsor?

Visibility: Gain exposure among leading AI and technology practitioners.
Recruitment: Network directly with top graduate students and postdocs.
Impact: Support the growth of our transdisciplinary AI academic community.



Sponsorship perks

Logo placement on the website.
Explicit mention in opening and closing remarks.

If your organization is interested in sponsoring the symposium, please contact us at transdisciplinaryaihub@gmail.com.

title: Call for Papers
text: We invite submissions for the TAIH Symposium 2026. We welcome contributions from researchers and practitioners across academia and industry.

title: Call for Papers
text:

title: Call for Papers
text:
Topics of interest
We welcome research on, but not limited to:

Explainability and interpretability
Fairness and bias mitigation
Trustworthy AI in healthcare
Robustness and security
Human-AI interaction
Ethical and societal implications of AI
AI in engineering and manufacturing
Case studies and real-world applications

title: Call for Papers

All submissions will be peer-reviewed in a double-blind process.
